subject: Meta/Facebook/Instagram child-safety & online-harms: litigation and policy record

cases.name: Frances Haugen disclosures / 'The Facebook Files' (whistleblower event, not a case)
cases.outcome: Internal Instagram-teen research disclosed; evidentiary basis for later suits; research was largely opinion-survey, methodologically limited

cases.name: Multistate AG action vs Meta (42 AGs; 33 federal in N.D. Cal., 9 state)
cases.outcome: Filed Oct 24, 2023; MTDs largely denied Oct 2024; ongoing

cases.name: New Mexico v. Meta Platforms & Mark Zuckerberg
cases.status: verdict Mar 24, 2026; Phase 2 (public nuisance / remediation, bench) began May 4, 2026
cases.outcome: LOSS for Meta: jury found Unfair Practices Act violations; $375M civil penalties ($5,000/violation max); first state trial win vs Big Tech on child safety; injunctive relief sought (age verification, private-by-default, addictive-feature bans, 90-hr/mo cap, CSAM detection, court monitor)

cases.name: In re: Social Media Adolescent Addiction/Personal Injury Products Liability Litigation, MDL No. 3047
cases.forum: N.D. Cal., Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers
cases.outcome: Nov 14, 2023 MTD: design-defect/negligence/failure-to-warn survive Section 230 & 1A; content-moderation claims barred. MTDs largely denied 2024. Mixed but net adverse for Meta.

cases.name: Breathitt County (KY) School District bellwether (within MDL 3047)
cases.outcome: LOSS-equivalent for Meta: settled as last remaining defendant (after Snap, TikTok, YouTube) to avoid June 2026 bellwether trial; sealed terms

cases.name: K.G.M. v. Meta Platforms & Alphabet/YouTube
cases.outcome: LOSS for Meta: jury found negligent product design; $6M total ($3M compensatory, Meta 70%/Google 30%; $3M punitive for malice/reckless disregard); first negligence verdict on addictive design

cases.name: CCIA (Apple/Google/Meta/Amazon) v. Texas (App Store Accountability Act, SB 2420)
cases.status: ongoing; injunction granted Dec 2025, lifted by 5th Cir. May 28, 2026 (law enforceable for now)
cases.outcome: Mixed: industry won district-court 1A injunction, then lost it on appeal; merits unresolved; possible SCOTUS

cases.name: CCIA v. Utah (App Store Accountability Act, SB 142)
cases.status: suit filed Feb 2026, dropped Apr 21, 2026 after Utah amended to private-only enforcement
cases.outcome: Suit withdrawn; law remains active (private-suit enforcement)

cases.name: EU Commission DSA proceedings vs Meta (protection of minors)
cases.outcome: Adverse-leaning for Meta: preliminarily breached DSA (under-13 access, weak age verification, addictive design); exposure up to 6% of global annual revenue

cases.name: UK Online Safety Act / Ofcom Protection of Children Codes
cases.status: Codes in force Jul 25, 2025; enforcement phase active
cases.outcome: Regulatory duties live; Ofcom checking compliance and opening investigations; Meta made grooming-protection changes

cases.name: Australia under-16 social media ban
cases.status: in force Dec 10, 2025; enforcement gaps reported 2026
cases.outcome: Adverse for Meta (lost under-16 segment); Meta complied (began blocking Dec 4, ~550k accounts removed) while arguing app stores should bear age-verification duty

policy_positions.position: Supports app-store-level age verification & parental consent (Utah SB142, Texas SB2420, ~20-state push); duty placed on Apple/Google app stores, not individual apps
policy_positions.beneficiary: Meta (shifts age-verification cost/privacy liability onto Apple & Google)

policy_positions.position: Oppose app-store age-verification mandates; characterize them as Meta 'offloading its own responsibilities'
policy_positions.beneficiary: Apple/Google (avoid OS-level verification cost & privacy liability)

policy_positions.position: Launched Instagram Teen Accounts (default-private under-16, parental permission to loosen) Sep 17, 2024
policy_positions.beneficiary: Meta (self-set compliance baseline pre-empting harsher external mandates; PR amid 42-AG suit and KOSA)

policy_positions.position: Lobbied AGAINST KOSA's broad duty-of-care; Zuckerberg declined to endorse KOSA at Jan 31, 2024 Senate hearing
policy_positions.beneficiary: Meta (avoids open-ended federal duty-of-care liability) — CUTS AGAINST 'embrace regulation as a moat' reading

policy_positions.position: Federal lobbying spend $24.43M (2024), $26.29M (2025); among top corporate spenders

COVERTLY funded the 'Digital Childhood Alliance' (DCA) — a coalition presenting as 50+ grassroots conservative child-safety orgs — to push App Store Accountability Acts (age verification at the Apple/Google layer). Bloomberg exposed the stealth funding July 2025. DCA domain registered Dec 18 2024, fully-formed pro site next day ('staged deployment, not a grassroots launch'); nearly every DCA post attacks Apple/Google and NEVER criticizes Meta. Meta also ran a 'momfluencer' astroturf operation (Tech Transparency Project). The viral '$2B' grant figure remains traced-but-uncorroborated (Reddit/HN OSINT); defensible documented spend is ~$26.3M federal lobbying 2025 (86+ lobbyists, 45 states) plus the covert DCA backing.
policy_positions.beneficiary: Meta (advances app-store-verification laws that move liability to Apple/Google while concealing Meta's hand) — THE 'BOTH SIDES' MOVE: covertly bankroll the age-verification push while publicly opposing KOSA's duty-of-care

policy_positions.actor: Plaintiffs' bar (e.g., Jayne Conroy, opioid-MDL veteran)
policy_positions.position: Explicitly applies tobacco/opioid public-nuisance & product-defect playbook to reframe addiction as design harm and bypass Section 230
policy_positions.beneficiary: Plaintiffs/states (litigation leverage); contested by AEI as a 'deceptively flawed' analogy

grade_reasoning: UPGRADED 2026-06-07 after verifying the 'both sides' move. The liability-STEERING sub-claim is now STRONGLY documented and shown to be DELIBERATE AND CONCEALED, not merely convergent: Meta (a) publicly lobbied (with Snap, X) across ~20 states (Utah SB142, Texas SB2420) and Australia to push age-verification duty onto Apple/Google app stores — Google called it Meta 'offloading their own responsibilities'; AND (b) COVERTLY funded the astroturf 'Digital Childhood Alliance' (Bloomberg, Jul 2025) — a staged pseudo-grassroots coalition that attacks only Apple/Google and never Meta — to drive those same App Store Accountability Acts; AND (c) ran a 'momfluencer' influencer operation to manufacture parent support; WHILE (d) simultaneously leading and funding OPPOSITION to KOSA's broad federal duty-of-care (Zuckerberg refused to endorse KOSA) because that bill would land liability on Meta. Funding the pro-age-verification side covertly while publicly fighting the bill that targets you is precisely 'sitting on both sides' to STEER liability toward rivals and away from itself. This is concealed coordination, which is stronger than convergent self-interest. WHAT IS STILL NOT SUPPORTED: the maximalist 'unified cabal amplifies ALL child-safety lawfare as one master plan' — Meta also sued (via CCIA) to block app-store laws whose enforcement provisions hit it, i.e., the steering is issue-by-issue liability optimization, not blanket pro-regulation. The '$2B dark-money' figure remains traced-but-uncorroborated (Reddit/HN OSINT); defensible documented spend is ~$26.3M federal lobbying (2025) + the covert DCA backing. NET: a deliberately concealed liability-steering operation (strong) embedded in broader opportunistic self-interest (not a single grand conspiracy).
